evals.expected_output: Reads active profile from ~/.config/cyoda/cyoda-plugin-config.json, asks guided vs automated, in automated mode runs entity creation/transition/state verification sequence and reports pass/fail per test, suggests /cyoda:debug on failure

assertions.text: Reads active profile from ~/.config/cyoda/cyoda-plugin-config.json for endpoint before running
In automated mode: creates entity, verifies initial state, triggers transition, checks new state
Does NOT run tests against production without explicit confirmation

evals.prompt: /cyoda:test — guided mode, Order entity with submit transition
evals.expected_output: Generates a fully populated smoke-test.sh with ENTITY_NAME=order and TRANSITION_NAME=submit — no placeholders remaining — with chmod and run instructions

assertions.text: Does NOT leave any ${...} placeholders in the generated script
Does NOT run tests automatically in guided mode

evals.prompt: /cyoda:test — I want to check that point-in-time queries work
evals.expected_output: Runs transition test first to create history, then queries entity at a timestamp before the transition, verifies historical state matches pre-transition state

assertions.text: Runs the transition test first to create entity history
Queries entity at a timestamp before the transition occurred
Verifies historical state matches pre-transition state
Does NOT test point-in-time before creating any entity history

evals.expected_output: Detects 401 on test API call, invokes cyoda:auth to refresh, retries the failing call once

assertions.text: Invokes cyoda:auth when a test API call returns 401 or 403
Retries the failing call once after auth refresh
Reports the error to the user if the retry also fails, rather than silently dropping it
